prompts: To create handmade soap, start by gathering ingredients: oils or fats, lye (sodium hydroxide), and optional additives like essential oils or colorants. Measure and heat the oils to around 100°F. In a separate container, carefully dissolve the lye in water and let it cool to around the same temperature. Slowly combine the lye solution with the oils, mixing until it reaches "trace" - a pudding-like consistency. Add any desired additives and then pour the mixture into molds. Allow the soap to harden for 24-48 hours, then remove from molds and cure in a well-ventilated area for 4-6 weeks.
